narr.Aqua Shard: Aqua Shard's traffic gap remains the headline: 22,717 users in W24, about 28% below the same FY week last year, and conversion has slipped to 5.2% from 5.9% LY, so GA4 bookings of 1,190 are well short of last July's 1,860. Google spend was pulled back to £1,351 (443 bookings, £3.05 CPA — efficient but volume down). Meta held 159 schedules at £15.96, above last year's £9.03. Walk-in signals fell to 341, though some of the latest week's Google conversions are still settling.
narr.Hutong: Hutong's users eased to 13,604, down 7.7% YoY, and the funnel stays soft: conversion of 4.9% against 6.1% a year ago left GA4 bookings at 663 vs 906 LY. Google ran £1,393 for 147 bookings at £9.48 CPA, still well above last year's £2.73. Meta improved to £13.83 on a strong 150 schedules. Walk-in signals recovered to 256.
narr.Regent St: Regent St (Kyoto + Nueva combined) is now converting broadly in line with last year at 4.2% (4.3% LY), but the traffic base has thinned to 6,257 users, about 19% below LY, dragging GA4 bookings to 263 vs 335. Google spend held near £1,015 for 61 bookings (£16.63 CPA). Meta sits at £17.16 on 59 schedules, still far better than last year's £25.71. The lever here is top-of-funnel volume.
narr.Azzurra: Azzurra held its traffic at 1,934 users, up 1.4% YoY, but conversion softened to 4.3% from 4.8% LY, leaving GA4 bookings at 83 vs 91. Google spent £625 at a high £32.89 CPA on 19 bookings. Meta scaled to £1,075 for 64 schedules at £16.79 — a big improvement on last year's £24.61. Walk-in signals were 38.
narr.Shiro Sushi: Shiro Sushi is volume-capped but converting exceptionally: 1,110 users (down 51% YoY) yet a 10.0% conversion rate against 4.5% last year, so GA4 bookings rose to 111 vs 102 LY. Google delivered 77 bookings at £7.60 CPA on £586 spend, and Meta ran at £10.34 — the cheapest in the portfolio. Walk-in signals were 182. Fixing the traffic shortfall is the single biggest opportunity in the set.
narr.DSL: DSL (Dim Sum Library) leads the portfolio on conversion at 8.7% on 6,218 users, with GA4 bookings of 543. NOTE: DSL rebranded, so the 2025 lines are pre-rebrand (Luci) and are NOT a like-for-like comparison — the apparent YoY gains are a rebrand artefact and last year's Google data does not exist on this account. Google spend was £275 in W24 (32 bookings, £8.60 CPA) while Meta ran £1,791 for 93 schedules (£19.26). No Google local-action conversions are configured, so walk-in signals show 0.

Aqua Shard: Latest-week users down 27.6% vs same FY week last year; conversion rate also down
Hutong: Users down 7.7% YoY and conversion rate down to 4.9% from 6.1%
Regent St: Latest-week users down 18.8% YoY despite conversion rate in line with last year
Azzurra: Users up 1.4% YoY but conversion rate down to 4.3% from 4.8%
Shiro Sushi: Latest-week users down 51.3% YoY — volume-capped, though conversion is double last year
DSL: Pre-rebrand (Luci) baseline last year — YoY lines are not a like-for-like comparison

sharednote.Regent St: Regent St combines Aqua Kyoto + Aqua Nueva (GA4 users/bookings & Meta spend/schedules summed; Google is a single shared account — Kyoto + Nueva booking actions summed). Walk-in signals are the shared Google account's local actions (Directions + Menu views + Clicks to call; no Store-visit action on this account).
sharednote.Hutong: Google bookings sum two booking actions ('Sevenrooms Booking Complete' + 'Hutong UK - GA4 (web) sevenrooms_booking_complete'). No 'Click Book Now' conversion action exists on this account, so walk-in signals cover Store visits + Directions + Menu views + Clicks to call.
sharednote.Shiro Sushi: Google bookings sum three booking actions (SST booking_complete + UK sevenrooms_booking_complete + Booking Complete).
sharednote.Azzurra: Google account has no Store-visit conversion; walk-in signals cover Directions + Menu views + Clicks to call only, and no local actions were recorded last year (LY = 0).
sharednote.DSL: DSL Meta account reports as 'Luci' (confirmed mapping). Google account has no 2025 data (post-rebrand build) and no local-action conversions, so LY Google bookings/CPA and all walk-in signals show 0.
